title: EU Sustainable Finance Disclosure Update
content:
TO: All Investment Advisors and Portfolio Managers
FROM: Compliance Department - Meridian Wealth Partners
DATE: March 15, 2025
RE: Updated SFDR Article 6 Disclosure Requirements and EU Taxonomy Alignment

This memorandum provides critical updates regarding our obligations under the Sustainable Finance Disclosure Regulation (SFDR) Article 6 and the EU Taxonomy Regulation, effective immediately for all client-facing activities.

Key Regulatory Changes:

Under SFDR Article 6, financial market participants must now disclose how sustainability risks are integrated into investment decisions and the likely impacts of sustainability risks on returns. For our Article 6 classified funds representing approximately 68% of our €2.4 billion assets under management, enhanced pre-contractual disclosures are now mandatory.

The EU Taxonomy Regulation requires disclosure of the proportion of investments that are taxonomy-aligned. Our analysis indicates that currently 23% of equity holdings across our core portfolios meet the technical screening criteria for substantial contribution to environmental objectives, while 34% satisfy the 'do no significant harm' principle.

Client Communication Requirements:

1. All investment proposals must include a dedicated sustainability risk assessment section. This applies to portfolios above €100,000 minimum threshold.

2. Quarterly reporting must now incorporate Principal Adverse Impact (PAI) indicators. Key metrics include carbon footprint (measured in tCO2e per million EUR invested), water usage, and waste generation statistics.

3. For discretionary mandates exceeding €500,000, clients must receive annual taxonomy alignment reports showing the percentage of investments contributing to climate change mitigation (currently averaging 18.7% across our strategies) and adaptation objectives (averaging 4.2%).

Product Classification Updates:

Our sustainable equity strategy (ISIN: LU2847291648) has been reclassified from Article 6 to Article 8 following enhanced ESG integration processes. The strategy now maintains a minimum 70% allocation to companies with positive ESG momentum scores and excludes investments in entities deriving more than 5% of revenues from controversial weapons or thermal coal.

The European Green Bond Fund (ISIN: LU2847291731) maintains its Article 9 classification with 94% of holdings meeting EU Taxonomy alignment criteria as of February 2025.

Operational Implementation:

Effective April 1, 2025, all client onboarding procedures must include sustainability preference questionnaires. Clients must explicitly state whether they wish to consider principal adverse impacts on sustainability factors in their investment strategy.

Risk management systems have been updated to incorporate climate scenario analysis using Representative Concentration Pathway (RCP) models. Portfolio stress testing now includes 1.5°C, 2°C, and 3°C warming scenarios with corresponding impact assessments on sector allocations.

Compliance Monitoring:

Monthly compliance reviews will verify that sustainability risk disclosures are current and accurate. Any material changes to taxonomy alignment percentages exceeding ±5% must be reported within 10 business days.

Training sessions on updated SFDR requirements are scheduled for March 28-30, 2025. Attendance is mandatory for all client-facing staff. Certification testing will occur by April 15, 2025.

For questions regarding specific client situations or technical taxonomy calculations, contact the Compliance Department at compliance@meridianwp.eu or extension 2847.

This update supersedes previous guidance dated January 2025. Additional clarifications will follow as regulatory technical standards evolve.
